title: EMERGENCY RENT ASSISTANCE PROGRAM - 211info
text: EL PROGRAMA HISPANO CATÓLICO EMERGENCY RENT ASSISTANCE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides rent payment assistance. Assistance is limited to available funding and is not guaranteed. 333 SE 223rd Avenue Suite 100 Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Must meet all of the following criteria: -Be a resident of Multnomah County -Be at substantial risk of experiencing homelessness -Household income at or below 60% of area median income (AMI) Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-4:30pm by appointment only Email (503) 669-8350 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (503) 669-8350 Main phone (503) 232-4448 Hotline UNICA Crisis Line/Linea de Crisis UNICA Main phone (503) 669-8350 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information and to check if rent assistance is accepting appointments FEES: None DOCUMENTS: For rental assistance programs, all of the following are required: -Identification for all adults over the age of 18. Driver's licenses/state ID cards, Social Security cards, passports, or birth certificates are accepted forms. -Proof of income such as a pay stub, or Supplemental Security Income (SSI), Social Security Disability Insurance (SSDI), child support, TANF award letters. A personal statement may also be accepted on a case by case basis. -Current leasing information -Copy of eviction notice or Section 8 letter, if applicable LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Multnomah County Services: Rent Payment Assistance Rent Payment Assistance * Hispanic/Latino Community Other Services Offered At This Location: FOOD - FOOD JUSTICE HOUSING/SHELTER - EVICTION PREVENTION HOUSING/SHELTER - HOME FINDER SERVICES INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - YOUTH AND FAMILY EDUCATION ENGAGEMENT PREK-12 INFORMATION SERVICES - INFORMATION AND REFERRAL LEGAL SERVICES - FAIR HOUSING ENFORCEMENT COLLABORATIVE MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - UNICA UTILITIES - ENERGY ASSISTANCE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. EL PROGRAMA HISPANO CATÓLICO
